title: Delivery Scam — Fake Tracking Alert
intro: A text claims your package is delayed and demands immediate action. Your goal: avoid clicking suspicious links and verify via official apps/sites.
advice: Don’t click unexpected tracking links in texts.
Open the official delivery app/site directly and check status there.
If unsure, contact the merchant/delivery provider using official channels.

d1.text: Delivery Alert: Your package is on hold due to address issues. Confirm now to avoid return. (link removed)

choices.label: I won’t click links. I’ll check the official app/site directly.
choices.explain: Correct: navigate directly to official channels.

choices.label: Click the link and enter my address details.
choices.explain: Compromised: phishing pages steal your data.

choices.label: Which carrier is this?
choices.explain: Okay but still risky: they can lie. Better to verify independently.

d2.text: You open the official site/app and see no issue. The text was likely a scam.

choices.label: Report the message as spam and delete it.
Ignore it and move on.
choices.explain: Fine. Reporting is better, but ignoring is still safe here.

d3.text: Carrier: ExpressShip. You must confirm within 15 minutes or it will be returned. (link removed)

choices.label: I’m not clicking anything. I’ll check my order confirmation and official tracking page.
choices.explain: Correct: don’t let pressure override safe behavior.

choices.label: Okay, I’ll confirm now so I don’t lose the package.

end_good.text: ✅ Safe outcome: You avoided suspicious links and verified through official channels.

end_bad.text: ❌ Compromised: You provided information through an unverified link. Assume the data is stolen and take protective steps.
